Dashboard Settings
Customize your dashboard's look, privacy and access
Money Symbol & Currency
Default Currency Symbol
This symbol will be shown on all financial KPIs and Charts
Preview: 1,234.56 (No symbol)
Changes here apply right away. They are saved permanently when you click Save & Apply Settings. All other visibility settings have been moved to the Features tab.
Password Protection
Add a Password
Anyone who opens this dashboard will need to enter this password first
Set an Expiry Date
The dashboard will stop working after this date. Leave blank to keep it open forever.

Master Access Rules
1. View Only Mode (Master Lock)
Forces all editing and interaction buttons to OFF automatically.
2. Allow Dashboard Editing
Use Case: Allow users to move widgets and edit charts.
Core Interactions
3. Allow Update Data
4. Allow Data View
5. Allow Save Dashboard
6. Allow Cloud Deploy
7. Allow Settings Access
Export Options
8. Excel Export
9. PDF Export
10. PPT Export
Advanced Data Logic
11. Allow Add Field
12. Allow Manage Fields
13. Allow Data Modeler
Creator Exceptions
14. Creator Only Access
Restrict admin controls to creator's device only.
15. Creator Override
Creator bypasses all view-only restrictions automatically.
User End Permission Summary
Users CAN:
  • Export to Excel
  • Export to PDF / PPT
Users CANNOT:
  • Edit Charts, Layout & KPIs
  • Update Data Source
  • Save Offline Dashboard
  • Deploy to Web
  • Access Settings Menu
Test your exact security settings without leaving this PC.
Hardware Level Protection
Lock Editing to This PC
Only you can edit or save this dashboard.
Your Device ID
XLBDNA_5F96WO
This is a unique ID for your computer. It is saved inside the dashboard file.
Extra Protections
Block Inspect Tool
Prevents viewers from using browser tools.
Secure Mode Only
Block Right-Click & Shortcuts
Always Active
Add Slicer
KPI: TOTAL SANCTION, KPI: TICKET SIZE +2 more
(Select All)
Update Data Source

Choose how you want to feed data into your dashboard. You can either connect a live cloud sheet or upload a static offline file.

RECOMMENDED
1. Live Google Sheets Sync
Use Case: Best for Teams, Live Dashboards, and Client Reports. Whenever you update your Google Sheet, the dashboard will automatically reflect the changes without needing any manual file uploads!
Don't know how to get the Live Sync Link?
Click here for the 4-Step Beginner's Setup Guide
Active (1h)
OFFLINE & PRIVATE
2. Manual Upload (Local Excel / CSV)
Use Case: Best for Strictly Private Data, Financial Reports, and Offline Use. Your data never leaves your computer. It is securely processed locally within your browser's memory.
Live Sync Setup Guide

This method turns your Google Sheet into a Live Database. Your dashboard will automatically sync and update without writing a single line of code!

1
Open your Google SheetGo to the Google Sheet where your data is stored. Ensure your data is formatted correctly (Row 1 must contain column headers/names).
2
Click File > Share > Publish to webFrom the top menu, click on 'File', hover over 'Share', and select 'Publish to web'.
3
Change format to CSV CRITICAL STEPIn the popup window, change the default 'Web page' option to Comma-separated values (.csv).
If you do not select CSV, the dashboard will fail to read the data correctly.
4
Publish & Copy LinkClick the 'Publish' button. A link will be generated. Copy that link, paste it into the URL box in the previous window, and hit Connect!

Publish Dashboard to Web
Need help deploying? Click here for the Step-by-Step Setup Guide.
Click here for the deployment setup instructions.
Host on Your Own GitHub Account

Host permanently on your own GitHub account for 100% privacy and full control.

Update Existing Dashboard (Optional)

Want to update an already live dashboard without changing its link? Paste its Full Live Link here (e.g., https://user.github.io/repo/Dash_123.html).
Leave this empty to generate a brand NEW timestamped link.

Deployed Successfully!

GitHub Pages takes 1-2 minutes to update the live site.

View Live Dashboard
Setup Guide & Use Cases
Publish to GitHub (Free Hosting)Ideal for Professionals, Businesses, and Private Data.
100% Secure & Private  |  Permanent Link  |  Full Owner Control
--- HOW TO SETUP OWN GITHUB (5 STEPS) ---
1
GitHub Username (Owner)Your 'Owner' name is simply your GitHub Username.
Example: If your profile link is github.com/john-doe, then Username is john-doe.
2
Create Repository (Repo Name)Click '+' (Top Right) on GitHub > 'New repository'. Enter a name (e.g., 'my-dashboard').
IMPORTANT Select PUBLIC and check Add a README file. Click Create.
3
Branch NameBy default, GitHub creates a branch named main. It is already pre-filled. Do not change it.
4
Create Live Link (Base URL)In your Repo: Go to Settings > Pages (left sidebar). Under Build/Deployment, select Branch: main and click SAVE. Wait 1 min, refresh, and copy the link 'Your site is live at https://...'
5
GitHub Token (Access Password) Click here to open Token Page
Name it 'Dashboard Upload'. Set Expiration to 'No expiration'.
CRITICAL Check the box named 'repo' (Full control). Click Generate and copy the ghp_... code.
SHUBLAKSHMI DASHBOARD

Smart Data Table Configuration
1 Basic Details
2 Table Engine & Logic
Pro Tip: Use Aggregated mode to group and summarize data. To display clean serial numbers that adjust automatically after aggregation/sorting, include the [S.No] pre-defined column from the list below!
Data Engine Mode
Aggregated vs Raw
Show Grand Total
Bottom metrics row
Wrap Text
Auto-fit multi-line text
Top/Bottom Limiter
Limits table rows based on ranking (Set to 0 to show all)
BY
3 Column Architecture
Check to include a column. Rename inside the text box. Choose Calculation type.
Display Name
Data Calculation
[System]
Add Calculated Field Excel Engine Active
Calculated Column
Measure (DAX)
1 Field Name
This name will appear in dropdowns.
2 Excel Formula
View All Formulas
Type [ to quickly select a column. Start with =
Quick Formula Templates
Logic (IF/OR)=IF(OR([Qty]>10, [Price]>100), 1, 0)Check multiple conditions easily.
Text Join=CONCATENATE([City], " - ", [State])Merge two columns with a hyphen.
Math & Rounding=ROUND([Revenue] - [Cost], 2)Calculate profit and round to 2 decimals.
Date Extract=YEAR([Order Date])Extract the Year from a date column.
Manage Calculated Fields
Excel Formula Library & Guide
This dashboard is powered by a full Excel-compatible engine. You can use almost ANY Excel function you know!
Math & Logic
Perform calculations and logical checks.
= [Revenue] - [Cost] = ROUND([Profit] / [Sales], 2) = IF([Age] >= 18, "Adult", "Minor") = IFERROR([A] / [B], 0)
Text Manipulations
Combine, extract, or format text strings.
= UPPER([City]) = CONCATENATE([First], " ", [Last]) = LEFT([Product Code], 4) = SUBSTITUTE([Phone], "-", "")
Date & Time
Extract date parts or calculate differences.
= YEAR([Order Date]) = MONTH([Order Date]) = DAYS([End Date], [Start Date])
Advanced Logic
Use AND/OR for complex checks.
= IF(AND([Qty]>0, [Price]>10), 1, 0) = IF(OR([Status]="A", [Status]="B"), "OK") = ISBLANK([Email])

Visual Data Modeler

How to use: Click and hold a blue table header to move it. To connect data, click and drag a column name from your Main Table and drop it exactly onto the matching column in your Lookup Table.

Active Relationships (Manage & Delete below)
No relationships defined yet. Drag a column from the Main Fact Table to a Lookup Table.
Beginner's Guide: Data Modeler

Welcome to the Visual Data Modeler! This tool lets you connect multiple Excel sheets or tables together so they act as one massive database.

How to Connect (Drag & Drop)

Simply click and hold a column (like CustomerID) from your Main Table, and drag it over to the matching column in another table.

Understanding Relationships

One-to-Many ( 1 : * )
One row in Table A matches multiple rows in Table B (e.g., One Customer has Many Orders). This is the safest and most standard relationship.
Many-to-Many ( * : * )
Multiple rows in Table A match multiple rows in Table B. Example: Many Students enrolled in Many Courses. The engine will automatically bridge these rows to ensure you don't lose data, but it multiplies the rows (creates combinations).

Initializing DuckDB Engine

Loading WebAssembly module...

First-time load only. Subsequent queries are instant.
